pr.title: Start sysdolphin's controller, add header w/ struct, match first 2 functions
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Started decompilation of sysdolphin/baselib/controller by adding controller.c and controller.h, matching the first two controller functions, and naming the main HSD pad globals throughout assembly references. The PR also introduced a minimal Dolphin PADStatus header and wired the new controller source object into the build. Most of the large diff is mechanical symbol replacement from lbl_804C1F78/lbl_804C1FAC/lbl_804C20BC/lbl_804C21CC to HSD_PadLibData/HSD_PadMasterStatus/HSD_PadCopyStatus/HSD_PadRumbleData across many asm files.
what_changed: Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.c with matched implementations of HSD_PadGetRawQueueCount and HSD_PadGetResetSwitch.
Removed the corresponding two assembly implementations from asm/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.s so the new C object supplies those symbols.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.h defining HSD_FlushType, RumbleCommand, HSD_Rumble, HSD_RumbleData, HSD_PadRumbleListData, HSD_PadData, HSD_PadStatus, and PadLibData.
Added include/dolphin/pad/pad.h with a minimal PADStatus definition containing button, stick, substick, trigger, analog, and err fields.

Renamed/commented four controller-related BSS globals in variables.h and controller.s: lbl_804C1F78 to HSD_PadLibData, lbl_804C1FAC to HSD_PadMasterStatus, lbl_804C20BC to HSD_PadCopyStatus, and lbl_804C21CC to HSD_PadRumbleData.
Propagated the named pad globals into many assembly files that index pad status or rumble data, including game-mode, fighter, menu, stage, debug, camera, and text assembly files.
Updated include/functions.h comment from func_803769D8 to HSD_PadGetResetSwitch while HSD_PadGetRawQueueCount was already named in the surrounding declarations.
Added src/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.o to obj_files.mk and e_files.mk near the existing asm sysdolphin controller object.
smart_moves: Matched small leaf/bootstrap functions first before attempting the rest of the controller module, reducing asm size while establishing the module's types and globals.
Used the observed BSS layout to infer a coherent PadLibData structure: qcount at offset 0x03 supports HSD_PadGetRawQueueCount, and reset_switch at offset 0x2B supports HSD_PadGetResetSwitch.

Kept the remaining unmatched controller code in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.s while adding a source object before it in the object list, allowing incremental decompilation without requiring the whole file to be converted.
Named shared globals globally rather than only inside controller.s, making later decomp work in fighter, menu, debug, rumble, and game-mode code easier to read.
Introduced a Dolphin PADStatus header separately from HSD_PadStatus, preserving the distinction between raw Dolphin pad data and HSD-normalized pad status.
decomp_lessons: For baselib modules with many cross-module data users, naming globals can be as useful as matching functions because it exposes array stride and field-offset meaning across the whole codebase.

A small matched function can validate struct offsets: HSD_PadGetRawQueueCount reads PadLibData.qcount under OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts, while HSD_PadGetResetSwitch reads PadLibData.reset_switch and normalizes it to TRUE/FALSE.
When migrating only the first functions of an assembly file, remove just those labels from the asm file and add a matching C object beside the remaining asm object in build lists.
Pad status arrays appear to use a 0x44-byte stride in many assembly sites, consistent with HSD_PadStatus as defined in controller.h.
Rumble data references can be separated from pad status arrays by naming HSD_PadRumbleData independently, even though related code often indexes all of them by controller port.
naming_conventions: HSD_PadGetResetSwitch for the former func_803769D8 at 0x803769D8.
HSD_PadLibData for the former lbl_804C1F78 BSS object.
HSD_PadMasterStatus for the former lbl_804C1FAC BSS object.
HSD_PadCopyStatus for the former lbl_804C20BC BSS object.
HSD_PadRumbleData for the former lbl_804C21CC BSS object.
HSD-prefixed typedefs are used for baselib controller abstractions, while PADStatus remains a Dolphin SDK-style type.
The added header uses internal tagged type names such as struct _HSD_PadStatus and typedef names such as HSD_PadStatus.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: HSD_PadGetRawQueueCount matches by storing HSD_PadLibData.qcount while interrupts are disabled, then restoring interrupts before returning the u8 count.
HSD_PadGetResetSwitch matches the original branch shape semantically as a ternary returning TRUE when reset_switch is nonzero and FALSE otherwise.
Assembly references were updated from anonymous labels to named globals using the same @ha/@l relocations, preserving generated addresses while improving symbol readability.

BSS labels in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.s were renamed in place with identical .skip sizes: HSD_PadLibData .skip 0x34, HSD_PadMasterStatus .skip 0x110, HSD_PadCopyStatus .skip 0x110, and HSD_PadRumbleData .skip 0x114.
Repeated mulli-by-0x44 access patterns across asm files were tied to HSD_PadStatus arrays.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue matching asm/sysdolphin/baselib/controller.s after HSD_PadGetResetSwitch.
Search for refinements to PadLibData, HSD_PadStatus, and HSD_RumbleData field names or sizes.
Check whether include/dolphin/pad/pad.h was later expanded to match the Dolphin SDK PADStatus header more fully.
Look for later review or commits that convert HSD_PadMasterStatus and HSD_PadCopyStatus extern declarations from comments to typed declarations.
Search for HSD_PadRumbleData usage in later rumble decompilation work.
